title: corydalis caseana subsp. brandegeei
intro: Corydalis caseana subsp. brandegeei, also known as Yunnan Corydalis, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae), specifically a variety of Corydalis that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds such as corydaline and protopine. The medicinal properties of Corydalis caseana subsp. brandegeei have been reported to provide relief from pain and inflammation, as well as promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. In herbal preparations, Corydalis caseana subsp. brandegeei is often combined with other plants, such as passionflower or valerian root, to create teas, tinctures, or capsules for the treatment of various health conditions.
